title: atractylis arbuscula subsp. schizogynophylla
intro: Atractylis arbuscula subsp. schizogynophylla, commonly known as a type of thistle, is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine.
